diffCoverage.notes: Reviewed the double-render risk that enabling self_listen introduces (same-device send echo) against the existing store + frontend dedupe.

findings.evidence: With self_listen on, the listener also redelivers messages sent FROM this app (same-device echo). Both dedupe seams handle it: store save_message skips an outgoing row with same account+thread+body, different msg_id, within ECHO_WINDOW_MS=15000 (plus the (account_id,msg_id) PK ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), and the frontend appendToBucket reconciles an outgoing echo into the optimistic just-sent bubble (adopting the authoritative id). A message from ANOTHER device has no optimistic bubble, so it correctly appends as a new outgoing bubble. Self messages do not bump unread (persist_incoming bump_unread=!is_self; frontend bumpUnread=!isSelf). Live smoke confirmed: self-from-another-device renders once; self-from-this-app renders once.
findings.fix: Accepted pre-existing limitation (from fix-outgoing-echo-dupe): two intentional identical-text sends to one thread within 15s collapse to one. Not introduced or worsened by this change.

summary: Reliability review: enabling self_listen adds same-device echo traffic, which the existing bounded, deterministic store + frontend dedupe already collapse; other-device self messages append correctly as outgoing bubbles and never inflate unread counts. The only trade-off (identical rapid sends collapsing within 15s) is a pre-existing accepted design, not a regression. No blocking findings; pass.
